name: SSWING
address: 245 Park Avenue, E 47th St Level Retail, New York, NY 10167
about: SSWING is an indoor golf improvement facility located in New York City. They offer a data-driven approach to golf training using biomechanics and math-based learning. The venue provides access to advanced Uneekor simulators for practice and lessons. SSWING caters to golfers of all skill levels, with a focus on measurable improvement through personalized coaching and movement analysis. Their approach integrates physical training, club fitting, and technology to enhance performance.
why_golfers_like_it: Advanced Uneekor camera-based simulator technology for precise swing analysis
Biomechanics-focused approach with PGA professional and Doctor of Physical Therapy instructors
Custom club fitting services with SSWING-branded equipment
Data-driven, math-based learning system for measurable improvement

food_and_drink.notes: No mention of food service available on-site.
No mention of alcohol service or BYOB policy.

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Club rentals are not explicitly mentioned, but as a training facility, it's likely that demo clubs are available. Bringing your own clubs would be recommended for consistency in fitting and lessons.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The website does not mention any food or drink service, nor does it specify a BYOB policy. It appears to be a focused training facility without on-site concessions.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The website does not specify the time required for a full 18-hole simulation, but it does offer bay rentals and unlimited simulator use for members, suggesting practice sessions are the primary focus rather than timed rounds.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: SSWING is positioned as a serious training facility for golf improvement with a focus on biomechanics and data analysis. There is no mention of youth programs, family activities, or kid-friendly amenities, indicating it is not primarily oriented toward children.
